title: Foodie Play Kitchen Stove Wood Toy
summary: A toy stove with a plexiglass oven window and wood-round burner knobs — every joint gets reinforced, since this is a cabinet built to survive a toddler's daily use.
description:
The source is explicit that every joint on this build gets reinforced, and that's worth taking seriously — a toy stove gets more abuse per square inch than almost any piece of furniture in the house, between a kid climbing on it, slamming the door, and spinning the knobs. Building it square from the very first step and rechecking square at each stage isn't a nice-to-have here; a rack in the box shows up immediately as a door that won't close evenly.

The plexiglass oven window is what makes this read as a real appliance instead of a plain box with a door — screwed in with just one screw per corner, it's simple to install but adds real visual payoff. Wood rounds for burner knobs and scrap-wood "grates" nailed onto the top are both details that take minutes but do most of the work of making the stovetop legible as a stovetop.

materials.name: Wood dowel pieces, 1" long
materials.note: Leftover closet rod pieces work well.

steps.title: Build the basic box
steps.body: Build the box from the 1x12 top, sides, and bottom, using pocket holes or 1-1/4" brad nails and glue. Every joint on this build gets reinforced — a toy stove takes more abuse per square inch than most furniture in the house.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Attach the back to the sides, top, and bottom. Use shorter (3/4") nails here — longer nails tend to split out the sides and aren't needed just to tack the back on. Keep the whole project square as you attach it.

steps.title: Attach the front pieces
steps.body: Attach the front top and bottom rail pieces with 1-1/4" nails and glue, checking for square again as you go.

steps.title: Build and hang the oven door
steps.body: Build the door panel, ideally with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Attach the plexiglass window with 3/4" wood screws — just one in each corner is enough. Hang the door with self-closing hinges.

steps.title: Add feet (optional)
steps.body: Add optional feet to the base for a more finished, appliance-like stance.

steps.title: Build the stovetop
steps.body: Cut wood scrap into grate shapes for the stovetop and nail them down with 1-1/4" nails and glue. Attach the wood-round burner knobs and dowel pieces last.

images.alt: Wood toy play kitchen stove with plexiglass oven window
